agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The platform enforces scheduling integrity, physician credential verification, patient data privacy, and clinical documentation immutability — all critical for healthcare compliance (HIPAA-adjacent) and patient safety.","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Clinical Documentation","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled consultation between exactly one patient and one licensed physician, defined by a start time and end time. Represents the core transactional unit of the platform."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A physician whose medical license number has been verified by the platform. Only licensed physicians may accept bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling conflict where two or more appointments overlap in time for the same physician. This is a prohibited state."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The 2-hour period before an appointment's start time. Within this window, only a staff administrator (not the patient) may cancel the appointment."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"A platform user with elevated privileges who can cancel appointments regardless of timing constraints."},{"term":"Patient Medical Records","definition":"Protected health information belonging to a patient, accessible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician for a given appointment."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to a patient's appointment, and therefore authorized to view that patient's medical records."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the physician during or after an appointment. Become permanently read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment ends."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of validating a physician's license number before they are eligible to appear in the booking system and accept appointments."},{"term":"Lock Window","definition":"The 48-hour period after an appointment ends, during which consultation notes may still be edited. After this window closes, notes become immutable."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient may only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unlicensed practitioners to treat patients is a patient safety and legal compliance violation. The system is in a corrupt state if this occurs.","testable_assertion":"Given a physician whose license is not verified, any attempt to create an appointment with that physician MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments whose time ranges overlap.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking violates physical reality — a physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. Allowing this corrupts scheduling integrity.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, no two confirmed appointments may have overlapping [start_time, end_time) intervals."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their appointment if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., to 1 hour or 4 hours) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request succeeds if and only if now <= appointment.start_time - 2 hours."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Within the 2-hour pre-appointment window, only a staff administrator may cancel the appointment.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an access-control policy tied to a configurable time window, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a non-administrator role within 2 hours of start time MUST be rejected; the same request from a staff administrator MUST succeed."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient and their treating physician.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Unauthorized access to medical records is a HIPAA-level compliance violation. If any other actor gains access, the system has failed a core privacy invariant.","testable_assertion":"Any access request to a patient's medical records by an actor who is neither the patient nor the treating physician MUST be denied."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before they can accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a prerequisite gate for BR-1. An unverified physician appearing as bookable represents a corrupt system state.","testable_assertion":"A physician whose license_verified flag is false MUST NOT appear in booking searches and MUST NOT have appointments created against them."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Consultation notes become permanently read-only 48 hours after the appointment ends.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Clinical documentation immutability after the lock window is a medicolegal requirement. Allowing edits after this point compromises the legal integrity of the medical record.","testable_assertion":"Any write operation on consultation notes where now > appointment.end_time + 48 hours MUST be rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A physician with a verified license and no overlapping appointments","when":"A patient books an appointment within the physician's available time slot","then":"The appointment is created successfully and both patient and physician can see it on their schedules"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician whose license has NOT been verified","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The booking is rejected with an error indicating the physician is not available for appointments"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician who already has a confirmed appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"Another patient attempts to book an appointment from 10:15 to 10:45 with the same physician","then":"The booking is rejected with a scheduling conflict error"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A patient with a confirmed appointment starting in 3 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully and both parties are notified"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient with a confirmed appointment starting in 1 hour","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a message indicating only staff administrators can cancel within 2 hours of start time"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A staff administrator and a confirmed appointment starting in 30 minutes","when":"The staff administrator requests cancellation of that appointment","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A patient with an appointment assigned to Dr. Smith","when":"Dr. Jones (a different physician) attempts to view the patient's medical records","then":"Access is denied"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A patient with an appointment assigned to Dr. Smith","when":"Dr. Smith attempts to view the patient's medical records","then":"Access is granted and records are displayed"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An appointment that ended 47 hours ago","when":"The treating physician edits the consultation notes","then":"The edit is saved successfully"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An appointment that ended 49 hours ago","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is rejected and the notes remain in their locked read-only state"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A physician with a verified license and adjacent (non-overlapping) appointments at 10:00-10:30 and 10:30-11:00","when":"Both appointments are viewed on the schedule","then":"Both are confirmed — adjacency is not a conflict (half-open interval [start, end))"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Appointment booked exactly 2 hours before start time — patient cancellation boundary","expected_behavior":"The patient CAN cancel because the condition is 'up to 2 hours before,' meaning exactly 2 hours before is still within the patient-cancellable window (boundary-inclusive)."},{"scenario":"Physician's license is revoked after appointments are already booked","expected_behavior":"Existing appointments should be flagged for review or reassignment by a staff administrator. The physician should be immediately removed from future booking availability."},{"scenario":"Two patients attempt to book the same physician at the same time slot simultaneously (race condition)","expected_behavior":"Exactly one booking succeeds; the other is rejected with a scheduling conflict. This must be enforced at the data layer (e.g., database constraint or optimistic locking) to prevent race conditions."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes edit attempted at exactly the 48-hour boundary","expected_behavior":"A clear boundary rule must be defined: if now equals exactly end_time + 48h, the notes should be locked (>= 48 hours = locked). The system should use a consistent time comparison."},{"scenario":"Patient views their own medical records without any current appointment","expected_behavior":"The patient should always be able to view their own records — ownership access is unconditional regardless of appointment status."},{"scenario":"Cancelled appointment — can the physician still view medical records?","expected_behavior":"Once an appointment is cancelled, the physician is no longer the 'treating physician' for that encounter. Access should be revoked unless there are other active appointments between the same patient and physician."},{"scenario":"Staff administrator attempts to cancel an already-cancelled appointment","expected_behavior":"The operation should be idempotent or return an error indicating the appointment is already in a cancelled state."},{"scenario":"Physician has back-to-back appointments with zero gap","expected_behavior":"This is allowed if intervals are half-open [start, end). An appointment ending at 10:30 and one starting at 10:30 do not overlap."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Preventing double-bookings avoids lost revenue from scheduling conflicts and patient dissatisfaction. License verification prevents liability exposure from unlicensed practice. Consultation note locking protects against malpractice claims by preserving clinical documentation integrity.","operational":"Clear cancellation rules reduce support burden — patients self-serve for early cancellations while staff handle late-stage changes. License verification creates an onboarding gate that must be operationally supported. The 48-hour note lock window gives physicians flexibility while ensuring records are finalized promptly.","user":"Patients gain trust through transparent cancellation policies and guaranteed record privacy. Physicians benefit from conflict-free schedules and a reasonable documentation window. Staff administrators have clear authority for edge-case handling.","risk":"Primary risks are HIPAA-level compliance violations if medical record access controls fail, malpractice exposure if consultation notes can be tampered with after the lock window, and patient safety risk if unverified physicians can accept bookings. Race conditions in booking could undermine double-booking prevention if not enforced at the database level.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Protected by record privacy, scheduling integrity, and transparent cancellation policies. Trust in the platform depends on correct enforcement of these rules."},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Shielded from double-booking chaos and given a clear 48-hour window to finalize notes. Must complete license verification before onboarding."},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Given elevated cancellation authority for late-stage changes. Must be properly authenticated and authorized to prevent abuse."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Relies on consultation note immutability and medical record access controls for regulatory compliance and malpractice defense."},{"group":"Platform Operator","impact":"Liable for any breach of medical record access controls or allowing unlicensed practitioners. Correct implementation is existential for the business."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is well-defined with explicit rules, clear actors, and specific time thresholds. All six rules are unambiguous and internally consistent. Confidence is not higher because several operationally important details are unspecified (see low-confidence areas), but these are implementation details rather than contradictions or ambiguities in the stated rules.","high_confidence_areas":["Double-booking prevention rule is clear and enforceable","Cancellation policy has explicit time threshold and role-based authority","Medical record access control has clear actor constraints","Consultation note locking has an explicit 48-hour window","License verification is a hard prerequisite for booking"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the 2-hour cancellation boundary is inclusive or exclusive (assumed inclusive based on 'up to 2 hours')","How physician record access is affected by appointment cancellation","Whether patients can always view their own records independent of appointments","What happens to existing appointments when a physician's license is revoked","Whether the 48-hour lock boundary is inclusive or exclusive","Appointment duration constraints — no minimum or maximum specified","Whether staff administrators can also create appointments or only cancel them"],"assumptions":["Appointment time intervals are half-open [start, end) for overlap detection","The 2-hour cancellation window boundary is inclusive for the patient (exactly 2 hours before = still cancellable by patient)","Patient access to their own records is unconditional and not limited to active appointments","The 48-hour note lock is a hard cutoff with no override mechanism described","Treating physician means the physician assigned to a specific appointment, not a broader care relationship"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
